items_en: 1. Reaffirms the importance of the effective continuation of the activities of the United Nations Relief and Works Agency for Palestine Refugees in the Near East in all areas of their deployment;

2. Expresses its appreciation to the Commissioner-General and to all staff of the Agency for their tireless efforts and outstanding work, particularly in view of the difficult conditions, instability and crises faced over the past year;

3. Pays special tribute to the Agency for the essential role it has played since its inception more than 60 years ago in providing vital services to ensure the well-being, human development and protection of the Palestine refugees and to improve their plight;
4. Welcomes the important support extended by the Governments of the host countries to the Agency in the performance of its tasks and their cooperation with the Agency;
5. Expresses its appreciation to the Advisory Commission of the Agency and requests it to continue its work and to keep it informed of its activities;

6. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Working Group on the Financing of the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime and its efforts to assist in ensuring the financial security of the Office, and requests the Secretary-General to provide the necessary services and assistance to the Working Group in the performance of its tasks;
7. Welcomes the Agency's six-year medium-term strategy, which took effect in January 2010, and the continuing efforts of the Commissioner-General

Encourage l'Office  Ã   renforcer  l'aide  qu'il  apporte  aux  rÃĐfugiÃĐs  de Palestine en RÃĐpublique arabe syrienne touchÃĐs ainsi qu'Ã  ceux qui ont fui dans des pays  voisins,  conformÃĐment  Ã   son  mandat  et  aux  plans  d'intervention  rÃĐgionale ÃĐlaborÃĐs pour faire face  Ã  la crise  en  Syrie,  et  demande  aux  donateurs  de  veiller  Ã  apporter sans dÃĐlai un soutien durable Ã  l'Office Ã  cet ÃĐgard ;

12. Se fÃĐlicite des progrÃĻs dÃĐjÃ  accomplis par l'Office dans la reconstruction du camp de rÃĐfugiÃĐs de Nahr el-Bared dans le nord du Liban et lance un appel pour que les travaux soient achevÃĐs dans les meilleurs dÃĐlais, que les secours portÃĐs aux personnes  dÃĐplacÃĐes  Ã   la  suite  de  la  destruction  de  ce  camp  en  2007  soient maintenus  et  que,  pour  soulager  les  souffrances  persistantes  de  ces  personnes,  les engagements pris Ã  la ConfÃĐrence internationale des donateurs pour le relÃĻvement et la reconstruction du camp de rÃĐfugiÃĐs palestiniens de Nahr el-Bared et des zones du nord du Liban touchÃĐes par le conflit, tenue Ã  Vienne le 23 juin 2008, soient tenus avec diligence ;

13. Encourage l'Office  Ã   poursuivre,  en  ÃĐtroite  coopÃĐration  avec  les  autres organismes compÃĐtents des Nations Unies, ses efforts pour prendre en compte, dans ses  activitÃĐs,  les  besoins  et  les  droits  des  enfants,  des  femmes  et  des  personnes handicapÃĐes  conformÃĐment  Ã   la  Convention  relative  aux  droits  de  l'enfant 12 ,  Ã   la Convention  sur  l'ÃĐlimination  de  toutes  les  formes  de  discrimination  Ã   l'ÃĐgard  des femmes 13 et  Ã   la  Convention  relative  aux  droits  des  personnes  handicapÃĐes 14 , respectivement ;

14. Prend  note  avec  satisfaction ,  Ã   cet  ÃĐgard,  des  initiatives  prises  par l'Office  et  visant  Ã   proposer  pendant  l'ÃĐtÃĐ  des  activitÃĐs  ÃĐducatives,  culturelles  et ludiques  aux  enfants,  y  compris  Ã   ceux  de  la  bande  de  Gaza  et,  constatant  les bienfaits de ces initiatives, engage Ã  les soutenir sans rÃĐserve, en dÃĐplorant que des difficultÃĐs financiÃĻres aient conduit Ã  l'annulation des Jeux d'ÃĐtÃĐ de 2012 ;
items_en: 8. Expresses its appreciation to the Agency for continuing its reform efforts despite the difficult operational conditions, and urges the Agency to continue to implement procedures that ensure maximum efficiency in order to reduce operating and administrative costs and optimize the use of resources;

9. Takes note with appreciation of the report of the Secretary-General on strengthening the management capacity of the Agency, 11 and urges all Member States to give a thorough review of its findings and recommendations, including the continuation of funding from the regular budget of the United Nations;

10. Endorses the efforts of the Commissioner-General to continue to provide all possible humanitarian assistance, as a temporary emergency measure, to persons in the region who are displaced and in great need of continued assistance as a result of the recent crises affecting the Agency's sectors of activity;

11. Encourages the Agency to strengthen its assistance to the Palestine refugees in the affected Syrian Arab Republic and to those who have fled to neighbouring countries, in accordance with its mandate and the regional response plans developed to address the crisis in Syria, and calls upon donors to ensure the timely and sustained support of the Agency in this regard;

12. Welcomes the progress already made by the Agency in the reconstruction of the Nahr el-Bared refugee camp in northern Lebanon, and calls for the timely completion of the work, the maintenance of relief to the internally displaced as a result of the destruction of the camp in 2007 and the early implementation of the commitments made at the International Donor Conference for the Rehabilitation and Reconstruction of the Nahr el-Bared Palestinian Refugee Camp and the conflict-affected areas of northern Lebanon, held in Vienna on 23 June 2008;

13. Encourages the Agency, in close cooperation with other relevant United Nations bodies, to continue its efforts to integrate the needs and rights of children, women and persons with disabilities into its activities in accordance with the Convention on the Rights of the Child 12 , the Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women 13 and the Convention on the Rights of Persons with Disabilities 14 , respectively;

14. Takes note with appreciation, in this regard, of the initiatives taken by the Agency to provide children, including those in the Gaza Strip, with educational, cultural and playful activities during the summer months, and, recognizing the benefits of these initiatives, urges their full support, and regrets that financial difficulties have led to the cancellation of the 2012 Summer Games;

Demande  de  nouveau Ã   IsraÃŦl  de  lever  entiÃĻrement  les  restrictions entravant ou retardant l'importation des matÃĐriaux de construction et des fournitures nÃĐcessaires  pour  la  reconstruction  et  la  rÃĐparation  de  milliers  de  logements  de rÃĐfugiÃĐs  endommagÃĐs  ou  dÃĐtruits,  et  pour  l'exÃĐcution  des  projets  d'ÃĐquipement civils en suspens dans les camps de rÃĐfugiÃĐs de la bande de Gaza, tout en notant la mise en train de plusieurs projets en la matiÃĻre ;
20. Prie le  Commissaire gÃĐnÃĐral de continuer Ã  dÃĐlivrer des cartes d'identitÃĐ aux  rÃĐfugiÃĐs  de  Palestine  et  Ã   leurs  descendants  dans  le  Territoire  palestinien occupÃĐ ;
21. Note  avec  satisfaction l'achÃĻvement  du  projet  de  stockage  des  donnÃĐes relatives aux rÃĐfugiÃĐs de Palestine et sa contribution Ã  la modernisation des archives de l'Office ;

22. Note ÃĐgalement  avec  satisfaction le succÃĻs du programme  de microfinancement  de  l'Office,  auquel  elle  demande  de  continuer  d'aider,  en coopÃĐration  ÃĐtroite  avec  les  organismes  compÃĐtents,  Ã   stabiliser  la  situation ÃĐconomique et sociale des rÃĐfugiÃĐs de Palestine dans tous les secteurs d'activitÃĐ ;

23. Demande  une  nouvelle  fois Ã   tous  les  Ãtats  et  Ã   toutes  les  institutions spÃĐcialisÃĐes  et  organisations  non  gouvernementales de  maintenir  et  d'augmenter, outre  leurs  contributions  au  financement  du  budget  ordinaire  de  l'Office,  les allocations  de  fonds  rÃĐservÃĐes  aux  subventions  et  aux  bourses  Ã   octroyer  aux rÃĐfugiÃĐs  de  Palestine  pour  leur  permettre  de  faire des  ÃĐtudes  supÃĐrieures,  et  de contribuer  Ã   la  crÃĐation  de  centres  de  formation  professionnelle  Ã   l'intention  des rÃĐfugiÃĐs, et prie l'Office d'encaisser et de gÃĐrer lesdites allocations ;

24. Accueille avec satisfaction les  conclusions approuvÃĐes par un groupe de soutien de  l'Office  lors  d'une  rÃĐunion  extraordinaire  qu'il  a  tenue  le  26 septembre 2013,  en  marge  du  dÃĐbat  gÃĐnÃĐral  de  sa  soixante-huitiÃĻme  session,  et  demande  Ã  l'Office  et  aux  donateurs  d'assurer  un  suivi  rigoureux  de  ces  conclusions  en  vue d'atteindre les objectifs qu'elles fixent ;

Demande  instamment Ã   tous  les  Ãtats  et  Ã   toutes  les  institutions spÃĐcialisÃĐes  et  organisations  non  gouvernementales d'augmenter  d'urgence  le montant des contributions qu'ils versent Ã  l'Office afin de remÃĐdier Ã  ses difficultÃĐs financiÃĻres persistantes, croissantes et graves et Ã  l'insuffisance de son financement,
items_en: 15. Calls upon Israel, the occupying Power, to comply fully with the provisions of the Geneva Convention relative to the Protection of Civilian Persons in Time of War, of 12 August 1949; 5

16. Also calls upon Israel to comply with Articles 100, 104 and 105 of the Charter of the United Nations and the Convention on the Privileges and Immunities of the United Nations 3 in order to ensure the safety of the staff of the Agency, the protection of its institutions and the safety of its facilities in the Occupied Palestinian Territory, including East Jerusalem;

17. Urges the Government of Israel to promptly reimburse the Agency for all transit costs and other financial losses caused by delays and restrictions on the freedom of movement and access imposed by Israel;

18. Calls upon Israel, in particular, to cease obstructing the movement and access of Agency personnel, vehicles and supplies and to cease levying taxes, additional duties and charges, which adversely affect the activities of the Agency;

19. Reiterates its call upon Israel to lift fully the restrictions on or delaying the importation of construction materials and supplies necessary for the reconstruction and repair of thousands of damaged or destroyed refugee shelters and for the implementation of outstanding civilian equipment projects in the refugee camps in the Gaza Strip, while noting the initiation of several projects in this regard;
20. Requests the Commissioner-General to continue to issue identity cards to Palestine refugees and their descendants in the Occupied Palestinian Territory;
21. Notes with appreciation the completion of the Palestine refugee data storage project and its contribution to the modernization of the Agency's archives;

22. Also notes with appreciation the success of the Agency's microfinance programme, which it requests to continue to assist, in close cooperation with relevant agencies, in stabilizing the economic and social situation of Palestine refugees in all sectors of activity;

23. Reiterates its call upon all States, the specialized agencies and non-governmental organizations to maintain and increase, in addition to their contributions to the regular budget of the Agency, the allocation of funds for grants and scholarships to enable Palestine refugees to pursue higher education and to contribute to the establishment of vocational training centres for refugees, and requests the Agency to collect and manage those allocations;

24. Welcomes the conclusions endorsed by a support group of the Agency at an extraordinary meeting held on 26 September 2013, in conjunction with the general debate of the sixty-eighth session of the General Assembly, and calls upon the Agency and donors to follow up closely on those conclusions with a view to achieving the objectives set out therein;

25. Urges all States and all specialized agencies and non-governmental organizations to urgently increase their contributions to the Agency in order to address its continuing, growing and serious financial difficulties and the inadequacy of its financing,

text_en: in view of the fact that these financial difficulties have been compounded by the current humanitarian situation and instability on the ground, which have led to increased expenditures, including for emergency services, and to support the Agency's highly useful and necessary work in favour of Palestine refugees in all sectors of activity.
